local_names.romanized_local: Yang Rou Tao Can (Chinese), Set Kambing (Malay), Aattukkari Set (Tamil), Bhed Ka Set (Hindi)

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for 'Lamb Set' as a composite Indian-style dish. A0035 (Mixed dish with meat/poultry/game/fish/seafood) is the closest parent category for this multi-component meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of a Lamb Set, which includes Lamb Rogan Josh, Dal Tadka, Saffron Pulao, and Butter Naan, is approximately 300g.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb Set
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data directly from HPB Singapore Foodo for 'Lamb Set'. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water values were estimated from USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) using comparable lamb curry and mixed Indian meal entries, as HPB did not report these breakdowns.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Indian Mixed Meals — Analogous Estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 estimated from comparable Indian mixed meals containing lamb, dal, rice, and flatbread listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. A GI of 55 falls in the low category (≤55). The specific 'Lamb Set' composite dish was not found as a direct entry; the estimate reflects the blended GI of its components — protein-rich lamb and lentils lower the overall GI relative to the rice and naan components. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (55 × 6.29g carbohydrate per 100g × 300g serving) / 100 = 10.4g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 308mg/100g is borderline high; potassium at 254mg/100g provides partial offset but does not fully neutralise the sodium load at larger serving sizes.
hypertension.guidance: Request reduced salt in the curry gravy and limit portion size to one standard serving to keep total meal sodium below 600mg per sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate is moderate at 6.3g/100g with sugar at 1.6g/100g; protein at 8.2g/100g and dietary fibre at 1.3g/100g moderate postprandial glucose response; estimated GI of 55 is at the low boundary.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Substitute white rice with brown rice or reduce the naan portion to lower the overall meal glycaemic load, and avoid high-GI condiments or sugary drinks alongside the set.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lamb is a red meat with estimated saturated fat of approximately 2.9g/100g and cholesterol of 29mg/100g, both of which contribute to LDL elevation when consumed in excess.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Limit to one serving per occasion, trim visible fat from the lamb, and request the curry to be prepared with less ghee to reduce saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is a red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways central to RA pathogenesis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting lamb with fish or chicken, and request turmeric-based curry sauces to retain anti-inflammatory spice benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Lamb Set is a popular Indian-inspired set meal widely available in Singaporean restaurants and food courts, comprising multiple components served together as a complete meal. It typically includes Lamb Rogan Josh — a rich, aromatic lamb curry slow-cooked with yogurt, ginger, garlic, and whole spices such as cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon — alongside Dal Tadka (tempered lentil curry), Saffron Pulao (fragrant basmati rice cooked with saffron strands), and Butter Naan (soft leavened flatbread brushed with butter). The flavour profile is robust and savoury with a moderate spice level. Nutritionally, the set provides 117 kcal per 100g, with 8.2g protein from lamb and lentils, 6.4g fat (including estimated 2.9g saturated fat), and 6.3g carbohydrate. Sodium is borderline at 308mg/100g, partially offset by potassium at 254mg/100g. The estimated glycaemic index of 55 places this meal at the low boundary, making it relatively suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when portion size is controlled. Cardiovascular disease patients should moderate intake due to saturated fat and cholesterol from lamb. Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should note that lamb contains arachidonic acid, which may amplify inflammatory pathways. The set is halal-certified in most Singaporean establishments and contains gluten (naan) and dairy (butter, ghee).
common_user_queries: What is in a Lamb Set?
Nutritional information for Lamb Rogan Josh meal?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo for 'Lamb Set'. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy using comparable lamb curry entries and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: Saturated fat (2.9g/100g), monounsaturated fat (2.5g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (0.5g/100g), trans fat (0.1g/100g), and water (76.0g/100g) were estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy using comparable lamb curry and mixed Indian meal entries, as HPB Singapore Foodo did not report these specific breakdowns for this record. Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB source for this dish and no sufficiently close approved-source analogue was available to provide defensible estimates. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on comparable Indian mixed meals from the University of Sydney GI Database; the specific Lamb Set composite was not found as a direct GI-tested entry. FoodEx2 code A0035 used as closest parent; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for this composite Indian set meal.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Corrected foodex2_match_type from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' to use only valid enum values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] — fixes G5 hard gate failure.
Iteration 3: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to match glycemic_index=55 (GI ≤55 is classified as low per standard GI classification) — fixes Q4 failure.

Iteration 3: Added all romanized aliases ('Yang Rou Tao Can', 'Aattukkari Set', 'Bhed Ka Set') to flat aliases array to ensure full consistency with aliases_by_language romanized entries — fixes Q8 failure.
Iteration 3: Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to 10.4 consistent with GI=55, carbohydrate=6.29g/100g, serving=300g.
Iteration 3: Updated snomed_ct_description to remove erroneous '(disorder)' qualifier.
Iteration 3: Strengthened notes to document all -1 fields and estimation basis per G3 requirements.
